summary: Legacy static Workbench assets are no longer served by tools/workbench-server.mjs by default. Next.js gates now verify the mounted App Router runtime, a mounted browser-events API writeback probe, and the primary frontend-acceptance closeout gate. Legacy static assets still cannot be deleted because the full browser-events closeout gate, scheduler writeback, and shell tests continue to depend on desktop.html/mobile.html/workbench.js/styles.css.

evidence: test/workbench-server.test.js: workbench server is API-only by default for page routes
test/workbench-server.test.js: workbench server can serve legacy static shell only when explicitly enabled

tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs.project_mount_root_when_legacy_disabled: 404 JSON: workbench pages are served by Next.js

acceptance_gate_dependencies.legacy_static_requirement: starts workbench-server with serveLegacyStatic: true and validates legacy desktop.html/mobile.html browser-event scenarios including lifecycle cleanup interactions
none; primary frontend-acceptance now runs against the mounted Next.js App Router runtime and reuses the existing artifact validator
compatibility-only legacy desktop.html/mobile.html content acceptance; no longer the primary closeout frontend-acceptance gate
fixture-level frontend acceptance assertions still cover legacy desktop.html/mobile.html routes plus mounted Next favicon compatibility
verifies scheduler dispatch writeback through legacy desktop.html and mobile.html
asserts desktop/mobile DOM bindings, workbench.js projection rendering, styles.css mobile and overflow invariants

next_served_route_replacement_gates.replaces_requirement: Next.js Workbench served route verified at /projects/ai-control-platform/ for desktop and mobile viewports
does_not_replace: tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs legacy lifecycle cleanup and timeout browser-event closeout scenarios

next_served_route_replacement_gates.replaces_requirement: Next browser-events mounted runtime and API writeback probe verified; does not replace the full browser-events closeout gate yet
does_not_replace: tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs legacy lifecycle cleanup and timeout browser-event closeout scenarios

next_served_route_replacement_gates.replaces_requirement: Primary frontend-acceptance closeout gate migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to the mounted Next.js App Router runtime with the existing frontend-acceptance validator and durable release evidence.
does_not_replace: tools/check-workbench-browser-events.mjs legacy lifecycle cleanup and timeout browser-event closeout scenarios

retirement.reason: The Next.js served route, primary frontend-acceptance gate, and mounted browser-events API writeback probe are now verified, but legacy assets remain acceptance-gate and rollback dependencies until full browser-events closeout, scheduler writeback, and shell gates move off desktop.html/mobile.html.
required_evidence_before_delete: Browser-events gate migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html lifecycle scenarios to Next.js routes or replaced by equivalent mounted-route evidence
Scheduler dispatch writeback browser verification no longer depends on legacy desktop.html/mobile.html
FRONTEND_REFACTOR_CONSTRAINTS.md and FRONTEND_MIGRATION_INVENTORY.md updated to remove fallback-retention requirements
test/workbench-shell.test.js no longer asserts legacy DOM/CSS invariants as current acceptance gates
tools/workbench-server.mjs and tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs no longer expose opt-in legacy static serving, or the opt-in route is explicitly quarantined outside public runtime
